AI summaries for support tickets

A pitch for adding GPT-generated case summaries to the support inbox so agents resolve tickets 30% faster.

01 / Problem

Agents read 4 tickets to fix 1

Average ticket touches three agents before resolution. Each agent re-reads the entire thread on hand-off — same context, different reader. The cost is real: the typical resolution time has grown 18% in the last quarter while ticket volume held flat.

Concrete: on Tuesday a single billing ticket was opened, escalated, and resolved over 47 minutes. 22 of those minutes were context re-reading.

02 / Solution

One paragraph at the top of every ticket

A GPT-4 summary auto-generates when a ticket is created and updates after every reply. Agents read the paragraph instead of the thread. The thread stays one click away — nothing is hidden.

What changes
Top-of-ticket summary
A single paragraph: who the customer is, what they want, what's been tried, what's blocking. Updates on every reply.
What stays
The full thread
No data is hidden. Agents who want the full context click "show thread" and see what they see today.
03 / Ask

Two engineers, four weeks, $3K of API spend

The build is small: an OpenAI integration, a queue worker that summarizes on ticket events, a UI block at the top of the ticket page. Four-week scope. Two engineers. Total infra cost in month one: ~$3,000 in API calls (estimated from current ticket volume × 800 tokens average).

If we land it by quarter-end and the 30% faster resolution holds, the time saved is worth ~$80K/quarter in agent capacity. Decision needed by Friday.
